meta.held_out: None of these strings appears in the grader prompt or its (zero) few-shot examples; leakage.py proves this mechanically.
meta.how_built: Hand-curated realistic free-recall answers over MCAT-style flashcards. NOT harvested from real user telemetry (we do not have a week of real study logs). Labels are unambiguous human gold and were fixed before any grader was run.
meta.record: (question, expected, student_answer, gold_correct). `expected` is the card's own stored answer -- the grader's only ground truth (named-source honesty rule). `answer_kind` records how the student answer was constructed.
meta.what: Held-out evaluation set for the LLM answer-grader (qt/aqt/llm_grade.py).

llm_note: graded by an LLM agent (Claude) as a stand-in for gpt-5-nano; the agent judged a blind copy of the set (gold labels withheld) using the exact llm_grade rubric — a real measurement, not a simulation.
